Our licensed technicians provide fast, effective treatments with transparent pricing and a satisfaction guarantee, ensuring your home stays ant-free year-round.
localContext: Canarsie's proximity to Jamaica Bay and its many older residential buildings create ideal conditions for ant infestations, particularly carpenter ants and odorous house ants. Whether you live near Canarsie Pier, along the Rockaway Parkway corridor, or in one of the neighborhoods near Seaview Park, our team understands the unique pest challenges facing this southeast Brooklyn community.

faqs.question: What types of ants are most common in Canarsie?
faqs.answer: Canarsie homes typically experience infestations of carpenter ants, odorous house ants, pavement ants, and pharaoh ants. Carpenter ants are particularly common in areas near Jamaica Bay due to the moisture-rich environment. Our technicians can identify the specific species and apply targeted treatments for maximum effectiveness.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to an ant problem in Canarsie?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service for most Canarsie locations. Our team understands that ant infestations can escalate quickly, especially during warmer months, so we prioritize fast response times to prevent the problem from spreading throughout your home.

faqs.question: What does the $350-$550 ant control service include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive ant control service includes a thorough inspection of your property, identification of entry points and nesting sites, interior and exterior treatment applications, and a follow-up visit to ensure complete elimination. The final price depends on the size of your property and the severity of the infestation.

faqs.question: Are your ant control treatments safe for families and pets in Canarsie homes?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products that are highly effective against ants but safe for your family and pets when applied correctly. Our technicians follow strict safety protocols and will provide you with any necessary precautions specific to your treatment plan.
